commit.message:
fix: Remove package-lock.json to fix pnpm lock file conflict on Vercel

Issue: pnpm-lock.yaml is out of sync with package.json when both
npm and pnpm lock files exist in the repository.

Solution: Remove package-lock.json (npm lock file) since this project
uses pnpm exclusively. This prevents Vercel from failing with:
  'ERR_PNPM_OUTDATED_LOCKFILE: Cannot install with frozen-lockfile'

Now Vercel will use only pnpm-lock.yaml for dependency resolution.

commit.message:
fix: gRPC URL must use grpcs:// protocol prefix

Without an explicit protocol, Bruno's gRPC event handler prepends
http:// to the URL, causing createInsecure() channel credentials to
be used. CVP on port 443 expects TLS and drops insecure connections
with status 14 UNAVAILABLE.

Change url from {{cvp_host}} to grpcs://{{cvp_host}}:{{cvp_port}} in
all 11 gRPC request files so that TLS credentials are always created
for non-localhost connections.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove duplicate `list-instances` entry from CLI help (#4542)

The `list_instances` command was registered twice:
- Once automatically via `@main.command()` decorator (as `list-instances`)
- Once explicitly via `main.add_command(list_instances, name='list')` in `__init__.py`

This caused both `list` and `list-instances` to appear in `pymapdl --help`.

Fix by changing the decorator to `@click.command()` so the command is only
registered once (as `list`) through the explicit `add_command` call in
`__init__.py`. Also removes the now-unnecessary `from ansys.mapdl.core.cli
import main` import.

commit.message:
fix(grpc): add protoPath to all gRPC request files

Each .bru file now specifies protoPath relative to the collection root.
This tells Bruno to load method definitions from the vendored proto files
instead of attempting gRPC server reflection (which fails against CVP).

Without protoPath, Bruno's startConnection() falls through to reflection,
which CVP does not support, resulting in:
  'Failed to refresh methods and method /X/Y not found'

Proto path mapping:
- inventory/     -> proto/arista/inventory.v1/services.gen.proto
- changecontrol/ -> proto/arista/changecontrol.v1/services.gen.proto
- event/         -> proto/arista/event.v1/services.gen.proto
- lifecycle/     -> proto/arista/lifecycle.v1/services.gen.proto
- tag/           -> proto/arista/tag.v2/services.gen.proto
- workspace/     -> proto/arista/workspace.v1/services.gen.proto

commit.message:
fix: downgrade permanent redirects (301→302, 308→307) to prevent browser caching

Browsers cache 301 Moved Permanently and 308 Permanent Redirect
responses indefinitely at the OS/browser level.  The Location URLs
emitted by maskproxy contain proxy-internal paths (/__sd__/…) and
localhost addresses that are session-specific.  If cached and the proxy
is restarted, switched to a different target, or the user opens a fresh
browser tab, those stale cached redirects cause redirect loops or point
to the wrong host.

Fix: in modifyResponse, downgrade 301→302 and 308→307 so the browser
always re-asks the proxy on the next visit instead of serving the
redirect from its internal cache.

commit.message:
fix: correctly handle ccTLD targets in computeRootDomain

When the target is a 3-label ccTLD domain like ynet.co.il, bbc.co.uk,
or domain.com.au, the previous heuristic stripped the first label and
returned co.il / co.uk / com.au as the "root domain".  This caused the
subdomain masking regex to match ALL *.co.il domains (not just
*.ynet.co.il), routing unrelated external sites (analytics, CDNs,
third-party trackers) through the /__sd__/ mechanism.  Those external
sites had their own redirect chains which, when processed through the
proxy with user string replacements, created redirect loops that
triggered ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS in Chrome.

Fix: add a knownSLDs lookup table of well-known second-level domain
labels (co, com, net, org, gov, ac, edu, …).  When stripping the leading
label from a 3-label host would produce a 2-label co.XX / com.XX style
suffix, the original hostname is already the registrable domain and is
returned unchanged.

Examples:
  ynet.co.il  → ynet.co.il  (was: co.il)
  bbc.co.uk   → bbc.co.uk   (was: co.uk)
  domain.com.au → domain.com.au (was: com.au)
  www.ynet.co.il → ynet.co.il  (unchanged — 4 labels, strips www)

Also fix the startup log to always print the subdomain masking scope,
including when rootDomain == targetHost (e.g. ynet.co.il, github.com).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add missing proto dependencies to resolve 'method not found' error

Three proto files were missing from the vendored tree, causing Bruno to fail
silently when parsing proto definitions and fall back to 'Using Reflection'.
CloudVision does not support gRPC reflection, so all requests failed.

Added:
- google/protobuf/descriptor.proto  — required by fmp/extensions.proto
  (imported by every Arista service proto)
- arista/configstatus.v1/configstatus.proto  — required by workspace.proto
- arista/imagestatus.v1/imagestatus.proto    — required by workspace.proto

Full import chain now resolves cleanly (23 files, 0 unresolved imports).
Updated docs/grpc.md with expanded troubleshooting covering all root causes
including missing environment selection and the 'Using Reflection' symptom.
